title: Exact Organization of Density Matrices and Entanglement Structure in the Kitaev Spin Liquid
authors: Chen-Chih Wang, Yi-Ping Huang, Sungkit Yip
abstract: We give an exact form of the density matrix of the spin-1/2 Kitaev spin liquid represented in terms of spin operators and study the entanglement structures of the Kitaev honeycomb model within the spin framework. We show that the density matrix is naturally organized by equivalence classes of string operators associated with the underlying gauge structure of the model. With the explicit form of the density matrix, plus the exact Gauss law of the emergent gauge theory and the exact 1-form Wilson symmetry in the Kitaev model, we demonstrate the existence of the underlying symmetry-resolved block-diagonal structure of the reduced density matrix, which gives rise to the extensive degeneracy in the entanglement spectrum. The block-diagonal structure is then proven to be responsible for the separability of the entanglement entropy into the gauge and matter parts. Furthermore, we extend the formalism to subsystems with an odd number of lattice sites, revealing a relation between the entanglement spectrum and the fermion parity that is seldom mentioned in the literature.
summary: This paper provides an exact derivation of the density matrix for the Kitaev spin liquid, revealing how the entanglement entropy splits into distinct gauge and matter components. It establishes the microscopic link between spin and Majorana fermion representations and explains the high degeneracy in the entanglement spectrum through the lens of emergent gauge symmetries.
main_problem: The paper seeks to derive an explicit, physically transparent form of the density matrix for the Kitaev spin liquid in the spin representation to understand the microscopic origin of its entanglement structure.
main_result: The authors derive an exact form of the density matrix and prove that the reduced density matrix is block-diagonal, leading to the separability of entanglement entropy into gauge and matter parts and explaining the extensive degeneracy in the entanglement spectrum.
method: The study employs an algebraic approach using Majorana fermion representations, Gutzwiller projection, and the study of string operator equivalence classes, alongside symmetry analysis of the Gauss law and 1-form Wilson symmetry.
model_or_system: The Kitaev honeycomb model, an exactly solvable quantum spin system featuring a spin-1/2 liquid ground state with fractionalized Majorana fermion excitations and an emergent Z2 gauge field.
key_observables: Reduced density matrix (RDM), entanglement entropy (EE), entanglement spectrum (ES), spin correlation functions, and fermion parity.
important_parameters: Subregion size (even vs. odd number of lattice sites), boundary length, and the flux-free sector (fixed Z2 fluxes).
main_assumptions: The subregion is assumed to be simply-connected, and the Z2 fluxes are assumed to be static (flux-free sector).
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows equivalent edge configurations and Wilson loops; Figure 2 illustrates the linking of Majorana fermions via gauge-field strings; Figure 6 shows 't Hooft loop operators; Figure 8 shows the duality between triangular and honeycomb lattices.
paper_structure: The paper begins by deriving the exact density matrix in the spin representation, moves to analyzing the block-diagonal structure of the RDM using gauge symmetries, extends the formalism to odd-sized subsystems, and concludes with a geometric derivation of the entanglement entropy components using lattice duality.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

FIG. 4. An illustration of the block-diagonal structure of the spin representation. An operator SD can be seen as a com- position of 2L−1 copies of Majorana systems MD with the identical set of endpoints EP(D). Therefore, an algebraic op- eration of SD is equivalent to that of 2L−1 copies of the corre- sponding MD, which makes the spin representation identical to the Majorana fermion representation.
